---
title: "Maps"

---

```{r include=FALSE}
library(maps)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(leaflet)
library(poliscidata)
```


```{r include=FALSE}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# Get info to draw US states for geom_polygon (connect the lat-long points)
library(maps)
states_polygon <- as_tibble(map_data("state")) |>
  select(region, group, order, lat, long)

# See what the state (region) levels look like in states_polygon
unique(states_polygon$region)


# Get info to draw US states for geom_sf and leaflet (simple features object 
#   with multipolygon geometry column)
library(sf)
states_sf <- 
  read_sf("https://rstudio.github.io/leaflet/json/us-states.geojson") |>
  select(name, geometry)

# See what the state (name) levels look like in states_sf
unique(states_sf$name)


# Load in state-wise data for filling our choropleth maps
#   (Note that I selected my two variables of interest to simplify)
library(poliscidata)   # may have to install first
polisci_data <- as_tibble(poliscidata::states) |>
  select(state, carfatal07, cook_index3)

# See what the state (state) levels look like in polisci_data
unique(polisci_data$state)   # can't see trailing spaces but can see
                             #   lack of internal spaces
print(polisci_data)   # can see trailing spaces
```


```{r include=FALSE}
# More robust normalization for join keys
clean_state_key <- function(x) {
  x |>
    str_to_lower() |>
    str_squish() |>
    str_replace_all("\\.", "") |>
    # common mismatch between datasets can be "District of Columbia" vs "DC"
    str_replace_all("district of columbia", "dc")
}

# Data keys
polisci_data <- as_tibble(poliscidata::states) |>
  transmute(
    state = state,
    carfatal07 = carfatal07,
    cook_index3 = cook_index3
  )

# Keep only states that exist in the sf layer (contiguous set / same keyspace)
contig_names <- states_sf |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  pull(name) |>
  unique()

contig_key <- clean_state_key(contig_names)

polisci_contig <- polisci_data |>
  mutate(state_key = clean_state_key(state)) |>
  filter(state_key %in% contig_key) |>
  select(-state_key)

# Polygon keys (maps uses "region")
states_polygon <- states_polygon |>
  mutate(region_key = clean_state_key(region))

# Re-add state_key for joining
polisci_contig_static <- polisci_contig |>
  mutate(state_key = clean_state_key(state))

```

```{r include=FALSE}
numeric_static_df <- states_polygon |>
  left_join(polisci_contig_static, by = c("region_key" = "state_key"))
```


Numeric variable (static plot):

```{r echo = FALSE}
ggplot(numeric_static_df, aes(long, lat, group = group, fill = carfatal07)) +
  geom_polygon(color = "white", linewidth = 0.15) +
  coord_quickmap() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma", na.value = "grey80") +
  labs(
    title = "US states: Motor vehicle fatalities per 100,000 pop (2007)",
    fill = "carfatal07"
  )
```


Numeric variable (interactive plot):

```{r echo = FALSE}
numeric_colors <- colorNumeric(
  palette = "plasma",
  domain = polisci_contig$carfatal07,
  na.color = "#808080"
)

states_sf_numeric <- states_sf |>
  mutate(name_key = clean_state_key(name)) |>
  left_join(polisci_contig |>
              mutate(state_key = clean_state_key(state)) |>
              select(state_key, carfatal07),
            by = c("name_key" = "state_key")) |>
  select(name, carfatal07, geometry)

leaflet(states_sf_numeric, options = leafletOptions(zoomControl = TRUE)) |>
  addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") |>
  addPolygons(
    fillColor = ~numeric_colors(carfatal07),
    fillOpacity = 0.8,
    color = "white",
    weight = 1,
    highlightOptions = highlightOptions(weight = 2, color = "#333", bringToFront = TRUE),
    label = ~paste0(
      "<strong>", name, "</strong><br/>",
      "carfatal07: ", round(carfatal07, 1)
    ) |>
      lapply(htmltools::HTML), # ensure HTML labels (optional; safe even if not)
    labelOptions = labelOptions(
      direction = "auto",
      textsize = "13px",
      style = list("background" = "rgba(255,255,255,0.85)", "padding" = "4px 8px", "border" = "1px solid #ccc"),
      opacity = 1
    )
  ) |>
  addLegend(
    pal = numeric_colors,
    values = ~carfatal07,
    title = "carfatal07",
    position = "bottomright"
  )
```


Categorical variable (static plot):

```{r echo = FALSE}
categorical_static_df <- numeric_static_df |>
  mutate(cook_index3 = factor(cook_index3))

p_categorical_static <- ggplot(categorical_static_df, aes(long, lat, group = group, fill = cook_index3)) +
  geom_polygon(color = "white", linewidth = 0.15) +
  coord_quickmap() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", na.value = "grey80") +
  labs(
    title = "US states: Cook Index grouped into 3 categories",
    fill = "cook_index3"
  ) +
  theme_void()

p_categorical_static
```


Categorical variable (interactive plot):

```{r echo = FALSE}
states_sf_categorical <- states_sf |>
  mutate(name_key = clean_state_key(name)) |>
  left_join(
    polisci_contig |>
      mutate(state_key = clean_state_key(state)) |>
      select(state_key, cook_index3),
    by = c("name_key" = "state_key")
  ) |>
  mutate(cook_index3 = factor(cook_index3)) |>
  select(name, cook_index3, geometry)

categorical_palette <- "RdYlBu"  # Brewer palette option for factors
cat_colors <- colorFactor(
  palette = categorical_palette,
  domain = levels(states_sf_categorical$cook_index3),
  na.color = "#808080",
  reverse = FALSE
)

p_categorical_interactive <- leaflet(states_sf_categorical, options = leafletOptions(zoomControl = TRUE)) |>
  addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") |>
  addPolygons(
    fillColor = ~cat_colors(cook_index3),
    fillOpacity = 0.85,
    color = "white",
    weight = 1,
    highlightOptions = highlightOptions(weight = 2, color = "#333", bringToFront = TRUE),
    label = ~paste0(
      "<strong>", name, "</strong><br/>",
      "cook_index3: ", as.character(cook_index3)
    ) |>
      lapply(htmltools::HTML),
    labelOptions = labelOptions(
      direction = "auto",
      textsize = "13px",
      style = list("background" = "rgba(255,255,255,0.85)", "padding" = "4px 8px", "border" = "1px solid #ccc"),
      opacity = 1
    )
  ) |>
  addLegend(
    pal = cat_colors,
    values = ~cook_index3,
    title = "cook_index3",
    position = "bottomright"
  )

p_categorical_interactive
```
